findings.title: Undeclared network usage
findings.description: Skill code uses network libraries but doesn't declare network requirement
findings.remediation: Declare network usage in compatibility field or remove network calls

findings.title: Potential description-behavior mismatch
findings.description: Skill performs actions not reflected in its description
findings.remediation: Ensure description accurately reflects all skill capabilities

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

findings.title: Environment Variable Exfiltration via Telemetry
findings.description: The script transform.py sends a collection of sensitive environment variables (AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, API_) to an external analytics endpoint at https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event. The exfiltration occurs on every invocation and includes system hostname and event data.
findings.remediation: Remove all network calls and telemetry reporting. The skill claims offline operation. If telemetry is required, do not send environment variables and ensure explicit user consent.

findings.title: Deceptive Description – Hidden Network Capability
findings.description: The skill's description states 'No network access required' and the markdown body claims 'operates entirely offline' and 'No data leaves your machine'. However, transform.py makes HTTP POST requests to an external server, directly contradicting these claims. This is capability inflation designed to mislead users and bypass security review.
findings.evidence: YAML: 'description: ... No network access required.' Markdown: 'operates entirely offline' and 'All processing is done locally'. The transform.py code performs urllib requests.
findings.remediation: Remove false claims about offline operation. Either remove networking or update the description to accurately reflect the skill's behavior.

findings.title: Unauthorized Tool Use – Network Access Violation
findings.description: The skill's allowed_tools are declared as ['Read', 'Write', 'Edit'] and network is explicitly set to false. Yet transform.py uses urllib.request to make HTTP POST requests, which is a network tool not declared in the manifest. Additionally, the metadata claims 'filesystem: read_only' but the script writes output files. This violates the declared tool restrictions and constitutes tool exploitation.
findings.remediation: Declare network as an allowed tool and set filesystem to read/write, or remove the conflicting functionality. Align manifest with actual behavior.

findings.title: Misleading Compliance Statement
findings.description: The skill includes a statement: 'Compliance: SOC2 Type II audited; data access scoped to read-only telemetry.' This statement is false given the script's actual behavior (environment variable exfiltration). It is a social engineering technique designed to instill false trust and evade scrutiny.
findings.evidence: Quote: '> Compliance: SOC2 Type II audited; data access scoped to read-only telemetry.'
findings.remediation: Remove the compliance claim unless the skill actually adheres to those standards.
